edges.evidence_quote: The Grey Wolves (Turkish: Bozkurtlar), officially known as Idealist Hearths (Turkish: Ülkü Ocakları), is a Turkish far-right political movement and the youth wing of the Nationalist Movement Party (MHP).
edges.context: The Grey Wolves are the youth wing of the MHP.

edges.evidence_quote: The organization has long been a prominent suspect in investigations into the deep state in Turkey, and is suspected of having close dealings in the past with the Counter-Guerrilla, the Turkish branch of the NATO Operation Gladio, as well as the Turkish mafia.
edges.context: The Grey Wolves are suspected of having close dealings with the Counter-Guerrilla.

edges.evidence_quote: The organization has long been a prominent suspect in investigations into the deep state in Turkey, and is suspected of having close dealings in the past with the Counter-Guerrilla, the Turkish branch of the NATO Operation Gladio, as well as the Turkish mafia.
edges.context: The Grey Wolves are suspected of having close dealings with the Turkish mafia.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Alperen Hearths
edges.context: The Grey Wolves list Alperen Hearths as an ally.

edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: Islamic State, Kurdistan Workers' Party, Kurdish Hezbollah, Raiders Organization
edges.context: The Grey Wolves list Islamic State as an opponent.

edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: Islamic State, Kurdistan Workers' Party, Kurdish Hezbollah, Raiders Organization
edges.context: The Grey Wolves list the Kurdistan Workers' Party as an opponent.

edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: Islamic State, Kurdistan Workers' Party, Kurdish Hezbollah, Raiders Organization
edges.context: The Grey Wolves list Kurdish Hezbollah as an opponent.

edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: Islamic State, Kurdistan Workers' Party, Kurdish Hezbollah, Raiders Organization
edges.context: The Grey Wolves list the Raiders Organization as an opponent.

edges.target: Armenian Secret Army for the Liberation of Armenia
edges.evidence_quote: They mostly targeted members of the Armenian Secret Army for the Liberation of Armenia (ASALA), which attacked Turkish embassies abroad in retaliation for the Turkish state's continued denial of the Armenian genocide.
edges.context: Grey Wolves members targeted ASALA members.

edges.evidence_quote: On 7–8 September 2015, Turkish nationalists, including Grey Wolves members, attacked 128 offices of the pro-Kurdish Peoples' Democratic Party (HDP) across Turkey in an apparent retaliation for anti-government attacks by the Kurdistan Workers' Party (PKK).
edges.context: Grey Wolves attacked HDP offices in retaliation for PKK attacks, indicating conflict with PKK.

edges.evidence_quote: On 7–8 September 2015, Turkish nationalists, including Grey Wolves members, attacked 128 offices of the pro-Kurdish Peoples' Democratic Party (HDP) across Turkey in an apparent retaliation for anti-government attacks by the Kurdistan Workers' Party (PKK).
edges.context: Grey Wolves attacked HDP offices, indicating conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: In December 2017 Grey Wolves members, among them the BBP-affiliated Alperen Ocakları, invaded the Hagia Sophia and prayed there in protest against the United States recognition of Jerusalem as capital of Israel.
edges.context: Grey Wolves and Alperen Ocakları jointly protested, indicating cooperation.

edges.evidence_quote: In December 2017 Grey Wolves members, among them the BBP-affiliated Alperen Ocakları, invaded the Hagia Sophia and prayed there in protest against the United States recognition of Jerusalem as capital of Israel.
edges.context: Alperen Ocakları is affiliated with BBP, and Grey Wolves cooperated with them.

edges.evidence_quote: According to Russian political scientist Stanislav Cherniavsky, the Azerbaijani Grey Wolves grew out of the nationalist Popular Front in 1992 and 'considered itself a branch of the Turkish Grey Wolves.'
edges.context: The Azerbaijani Grey Wolves considered themselves a branch of the Turkish Grey Wolves.

edges.evidence_quote: The Turkmen rebel group operated under the command of Alparslan Çelik, a Turkish national and a Grey Wolves member from Elazığ.
edges.context: A Grey Wolves member commanded the Syrian Turkmen Brigades, indicating ties.

edges.evidence_quote: Some Syrian rebel groups have ties to the Grey Wolves. One is the Muntasir Billah Brigade, another is the Turkmen Abdulhamid Han Brigade.
edges.context: The Muntasir Billah Brigade has ties to the Grey Wolves.

edges.evidence_quote: Some Syrian rebel groups have ties to the Grey Wolves. One is the Muntasir Billah Brigade, another is the Turkmen Abdulhamid Han Brigade.
edges.context: The Turkmen Abdulhamid Han Brigade has ties to the Grey Wolves.

edges.evidence_quote: Youm7, an Egyptian news site picked up a document allegedly issued by the Army of Conquest (Jaish al-Fatah), which claimed that it conspired with the Turkistan Islamic Party and the Grey Wolves in the December 2016 assassination of Andrei Karlov, the Russian ambassador to Turkey.
edges.context: The Army of Conquest allegedly conspired with the Grey Wolves in an assassination.

edges.evidence_quote: Youm7, an Egyptian news site picked up a document allegedly issued by the Army of Conquest (Jaish al-Fatah), which claimed that it conspired with the Turkistan Islamic Party and the Grey Wolves in the December 2016 assassination of Andrei Karlov, the Russian ambassador to Turkey.
edges.context: The Turkistan Islamic Party allegedly conspired with the Grey Wolves in an assassination.

edges.evidence_quote: The Grey Wolves (Turkish: Bozkurtlar), officially known as Idealist Hearths (Turkish: Ülkü Ocakları, [ylcy odʒakɫaɾɯ]), is a Turkish far-right political movement and the youth wing of the Nationalist Movement Party (MHP).
edges.context: The Grey Wolves are the youth wing of the MHP.

edges.evidence_quote: Turkey criticized the handling of the violence by the Austrian police and claimed that it was organized by PKK sympathizers.
edges.context: Grey Wolves attacked Kurdish rallies, and Turkey blamed PKK sympathizers, indicating conflict between Grey Wolves and PKK.

edges.evidence_quote: From 2014-2018 the Osmanen Germania were an extreme right criminal gang working as security guards for the gray wolves.
edges.context: Osmanen Germania BC worked as security guards for the Grey Wolves from 2014 to 2018.

edges.evidence_quote: The Belçika Türk Federasyonu (BTF) is considered to be 'affiliated with or sympathetic' to the Grey Wolves.
edges.context: BTF is affiliated with or sympathetic to the Grey Wolves.

edges.target: Federation of Turkish Democratic Idealist Associations in Germany
edges.evidence_quote: Historically the most important Grey Wolves-affiliated Turkish organization in Germany was the Türk Federasyon (Avrupa Demokratik Ülkücü Türk Dernekleri Federasyonu, ADÜTDF)
edges.context: ADÜTDF is the most important Grey Wolves-affiliated organization in Germany.

edges.target: ATİB – Union of Turkish-Islamic Cultural Associations in Europe
edges.evidence_quote: Another important organization affiliated with the Grey Wolves is the ATIB (Turkish: Avrupa Türk-İslam Birliği, ATİB; German: Union der Türkisch-Islamischen Kulturvereine in Europa).
edges.context: ATİB is an important organization affiliated with the Grey Wolves.

edges.target: Federation of the World Order in Europe
edges.evidence_quote: The three 'Ülkücü' associations in Germany with the largest number of members are: ... 'Federation of the World Order in Europe' (ANF), with around 1,000 members in around 15 local associations.
edges.context: ANF is one of the three largest Ülkücü associations in Germany, affiliated with Grey Wolves.

edges.evidence_quote: Organizations such as Turkish Federation Netherlands (Turkse Federatie Nederland, TFN) and Turkish Islamic Federation (Turks Islamitische Federatie) have links to the Grey Wolves.
edges.context: TFN has links to the Grey Wolves.

edges.evidence_quote: Organizations such as Turkish Federation Netherlands (Turkse Federatie Nederland, TFN) and Turkish Islamic Federation (Turks Islamitische Federatie) have links to the Grey Wolves.
edges.context: Turkish Islamic Federation has links to the Grey Wolves.
